releases.author: skztr
releases.description:
- another attempt at fixing "copy entire file"
     - fix: copy buttons not showing in tabs
     - issue 4: "copy current section" command
     - issue 9: conditional-per-viewmode
releases.name: v1.2.4 - fixes and features

releases.author: skztr
releases.description:
- fixes an issue where the "copy file" command/buttons did not copy
       the entire file when there were no headings in the file
     - fixes an isssue where file manifests were not stripped when only
       manifest-stripping was requested
     - add an option to ignore links when copying a section (outputting
       only the visible link text)
releases.name: v1.2.3 - optionally ignore links

releases.author: skztr
releases.description: fixes an issue wherein the "copy file" command/buttons did not copy the entire file. This may or may not relate to issue #5, but was found when looking into it.
releases.name: v1.2.2 - fix "copy file" button/command

releases.author: skztr
releases.description:
- adds an "always show" option
 - fixes an issue wherein the copy buttons was sometimes shown in the wrong place, or multiple times
releases.name: v1.2.1 fix incorrect placement of copy button

releases.author: skztr
releases.description:
Adds a "copy file" button and commands

 - new "Title" option for where to display copy buttons
 - new commands "Copy file" and "Copy file (raw)"
 - optionally skip metadata when copying whole file
releases.name: v1.2.0 - copy file

releases.author: skztr
releases.description:
- dependency version bumps (no known impact)
- add some missing tests - now technically 100% coverage on everything not involving obsidian itself; though that coverage is only "happens to touch lines" as opposed to "actual functional tests intentionally written for everything"
- fix: invalid tags prevented future valid tags in the same text span from being detected
- fix: gaps not being generated by SyntaxNodes which did not cover all text (no known real-world impact)
- fix: various test cases which were not testing what they were supposed to, particularly around trailing SyntaxNodes
releases.name: v1.1.2 minor bug-fixes

releases.author: skztr
releases.description: Minor tweaks to authorship information and capitalisation
releases.name: v1.1.1 - update authorship information

releases.author: skztr
releases.description:
rather than using a naive, mostly line-based parser to strip out
comments and tag-lines, switch to using remark (which Obsidian itself
uses internally). This gets rid of almost all known edge-cases such as
misdetecting %%comments%% and #tags within `<html attributes="">` and
`inline-code literals`. Known remaining edge-cases are now noted in the
README file.

This had many implications, including:

 - switching to jest, which has more support for different dialects of
   javascript (via Babel).
 - creation of many (though not complete) unit tests, to ensure
   edge-cases are handled (or in most cases, completely avoided).
 - refactoring the way various aspects of the sections are detected,
   mostly with the aim of making testing easier/narrower.
 - making use of the existing section/heading cache, which should make
   "headed section detection" faster and more accurate.
 - splitting the entire plugin into a lot more, smaller files, to make
   everything easier to reason about.

And of course, all of it was an unreadable mess which went through many
internal iterations, the majority of which were completely broken in
numerous and interesting ways. So this commit is a squash of all that
work, hopefully resulting in a basis that any future work can make much
smaller/incremental changes to.

This message was copied from Obsidian using the plugin itself.
releases.name: v1.1.0 - use remark markdown parser
